mnemonics: Segments Are Packaged Data (S.A.P.D.): Segments are the data units at the Transport Layer, often packaged with TCP headers for reliable delivery.
keyPoints: A segment is the Protocol Data Unit (PDU) of the Transport Layer, specifically for TCP.
It is created by encapsulating application data with a Transport Layer header, which includes crucial information like source and destination port numbers and sequence numbers.

Segments are responsible for enabling end-to-end communication between applications on different hosts, managing connection establishment, reliable data transfer, flow control, and congestion control.
commonMistakes: Confusing a segment (Transport Layer/TCP) with a packet (Network Layer/IP) or a frame (Data Link Layer).
Assuming UDP's PDU is also called a segment; it is technically referred to as a datagram.
